distribution.name: work_from_home_burnout_dataset.csv
distribution.description: 1,800 rows × 11 columns containing daily work-from-home activity and burnout indicators.

field.name: day_type
field.description: Type of day: Weekday or Weekend

field.name: work_hours
field.description: Total number of hours spent working in a day.

field.name: screen_time_hours
field.description: Total daily screen exposure time (work + meetings).

field.name: meetings_count
field.description: Number of meetings attended during the day.

field.name: breaks_taken
field.description: Number of breaks taken during work hours.

field.name: after_hours_work
field.description: Indicates whether work was done after official hours.

field.name: sleep_hours
field.description: Total hours of sleep obtained the previous night.

field.name: task_completion_rate
field.description: Percentage of planned tasks completed in a day.

field.name: burnout_score
field.description: Continuous score indicating burnout intensity (higher = worse).

field.name: burnout_risk
field.description: Burnout risk category: Low, Medium, or High.

name: Work From Home Employee Burnout Dataset
description:
This dataset captures daily work-from-home behavioral patterns and their relationship with employee burnout and productivity. It is designed to help analyze how factors such as working hours, screen exposure, meetings, breaks, sleep, and after-hours work collectively influence task completion efficiency and burnout risk.

The dataset consists of ~1800 daily records collected from multiple users, with each row representing a single day of work activity for a given user. Both weekdays and weekends are included to reflect realistic hybrid and flexible work schedules.

By combining objective workload metrics (work hours, screen time, meetings) with well-being indicators (sleep hours, burnout score, burnout risk), the dataset enables:
- Burnout risk classification
- Productivity vs well-being analysis
- Behavioral pattern mining
- Early burnout detection modeling

This dataset is suitable for:
- Supervised learning (classification & regression)
- Time-series trend analysis
- Workforce analytics
- HR analytics & occupational health research
